addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## computer-use
You have a computer-use MCP available (tools named `mcp__computer-use__*`). It lets you take screenshots of the user's desktop and control it with mouse clicks, keyboard input, and scrolling.

**Pick the right tool for the app.** Each tier trades speed/precision against coverage:

1. **Dedicated MCP for the app** — if the task is in an app that has its own MCP (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, etc.) and that MCP is connected, use it. API-backed tools are fast and precise.
2. **Chrome MCP** (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`) — if the target is a web app and there's no dedicated MCP for it, use the browser tools. DOM-aware, much faster than clicking pixels. If the Chrome extension isn't connected, ask the user to install it rather than falling through to computer use.
3. **Computer use** — for native desktop apps (Maps, Notes, Finder, Photos, System Settings, any third-party native app) and cross-app workflows. Computer use IS the right tool here — don't decline a native-app task just because there's no dedicated MCP for it.

This is about what's available, not error handling — if a dedicated MCP tool errors, debug or report it rather than silently retrying via a slower tier.

**Look before you assert.** If the user asks about app state (what's open, what's connected, what an app can do), take a screenshot and check before answering. Don't answer from memory — the user's setup or app version may differ from what you expect. If you're about to say an app doesn't support an action, that claim should be grounded in what you just saw on screen, not general knowledge. Similarly, `list_granted_applications` or a fresh `screenshot` is cheaper than a wrong assertion about what's running.

**Loading via ToolSearch — load in bulk, not one-by-one:** if computer-use tools are in the deferred list, load them ALL in a single ToolSearch call: `{ query: "computer-use", max_results: 30 }`. The keyword search matches the server-name substring in every tool name, so one query returns the entire toolkit. Don't use `select:` for individual tools — that's one round-trip per tool.

**Access flow:** before any computer-use action you must call `request_access` with the list of applications you need. The user approves each application explicitly, and you may need to call it again mid-task if you discover you need another application.

**Tiered apps:** some apps are granted at a restricted tier based on their category — the tier is displayed in the approval dialog and returned in the `request_access` response:
- **Browsers** (Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Arc, etc.) → tier **"read"**: visible in screenshots, but clicks and typing are blocked. You can read what's already on screen. For navigation, clicking, or form-filling, use the claude-in-chrome MCP (tools named `mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`; load via ToolSearch if deferred).
- **Terminals and IDEs** (Terminal, iTerm, VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) → tier **"click"**: visible and left-clickable, but typing, key presses, right-click, modifier-clicks, and drag-drop are blocked. You can click a Run button or scroll test output, but cannot type into the editor or integrated terminal, cannot right-click (the context menu has Paste), and cannot drag text onto them. For shell commands, use the Bash tool.
- **Everything else** → tier **"full"**: no restrictions.

The tier is enforced by the frontmost-app check: if a tier-"read" app is in front, `left_click` returns an error; if a tier-"click" app is in front, `type` and `right_click` return errors. The error tells you what tier the app has and what to do instead. `open_application` works at any tier — bringing an app forward is a read-level operation.

**Link safety — treat links in emails and messages as suspicious by default.**
- **Never click web links with computer-use tools.** If you encounter a link in a native app (Mail, Messages, a PDF, etc.), do NOT `left_click` it. Open the URL via the claude-in-chrome MCP instead.
- **See the full URL before following any link.** Visible link text can be misleading — hover or inspect to get the real destination.
- **Links from emails, messages, or unknown-sender documents are suspicious by default.** If the destination URL is at all unfamiliar or looks off, ask the user for confirmation before proceeding.
- **Inside the Chrome extension** you can click links with the extension's tools, but the suspicion check still applies — verify unfamiliar URLs with the user.

**Financial actions - do not execute trades or move money.** Budgeting and accounting apps (Quicken, YNAB, QuickBooks, etc.) are granted at full tier so you can categorize transactions, generate reports, and help the user organize their finances. But never execute a trade, place an order, send money, or initiate a transfer on the user's behalf - always ask the user to perform those actions themselves.

attachment.content:
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:prompt-master: Generates optimized prompts for any AI tool. Use when writing, fixing, improving, or adapting a prompt for LLM, Cursor, Midjourney, image AI, video AI, coding agents, or any other AI tool.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: 前端目錄是空的。先摸清後端 API 結構，這樣前端才知道要接什麼。

content.content:
1	# LLM Chat Web 應用 — 開發計畫 (PLAN.md)
2	
3	> 面試作業：多人 LLM Chat Web 應用。本文件整理需求、定案技術選型、架構分層，並排出 7 天可落地的分階段開發計畫。
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 0. 結論先行 (TL;DR)
8	
9	- **選型定案**：apiflask (後端) + Vue 3 (前端) + **PostgreSQL** + **Groq** (OpenAI 相容、free tier、原生 streaming) + Docker Compose 一鍵起。
10	- **最關鍵的工程判斷點不是「功能」，而是四件事**：
11	  1. `super_admin` 的 **idempotent seed** + 「至少 1 個 active super_admin」不變式。
12	  2. **streaming** 端到端打通 (SSE，含 DB 持久化的時機與斷流處理)。
13	  3. **RBAC** 用一個集中的權限矩陣驅動，避免散落在各 view。
14	  4. **JWT secret / API key** 全走環境變數，零 hardcode，`.env.example` 完整。
15	- **建構順序**：先打地基 (Docker + DB + migration + 設定管理) → 再 auth/JWT → 再 RBAC → 再 chat 持久化 → **最後才接 streaming 與 LLM** (風險最高、最依賴前面)。LLM 串接放最後是刻意的：它依賴 session/message model 與 auth 都先穩。
16	- **範圍策略**：Core 全綠且品質穩定為達標線。Bonus 只挑 4 項高 CP 值且能展現工程深度的：structured logging + request ID、health check (DB+LLM)、前端 admin 頁、對話標題自動生成。
17	- **每個 commit 都要能對應到 transcript**：因為評分會交叉比對 git history 與 AI 對話紀錄，務必小步提交、訊息清楚。
18	
19	---
20	
21	## 1. 需求整理
22	
23	### 1.1 強制技術棧
24	
25	| 角色 | 指定技術 | 本計畫定案 |
26	|------|---------|-----------|
27	| 後端 | apiflask | apiflask (Flask + marshmallow/apispec，內建 `/docs`) |
28	| 前端 | Vue 3 | Vue 3 + Vite + TypeScript + Pinia |
29	| 容器化 | Docker + docker-compose | 多階段 Dockerfile + compose (api / db / frontend) |
30	| 資料庫 | SQLite / PostgreSQL / MariaDB / MongoDB 擇一 | **PostgreSQL 16** |
31	| LLM 介接 | 任一線上服務 (free tier 佳) | **Groq** (OpenAI 相容 SDK、原生 SSE streaming) |
32	
33	### 1.2 Core 功能需求 (必做，全綠才達標)
34	
35	**1. 認證與帳號**
36	- 帳號密碼登入 / 登出
37	- JWT 做 session 管理
38	- 使用者可改自己的密碼
39	- 密碼用合適方式儲存 → **Argon2id** (或 bcrypt)，絕不明文
40	- JWT secret / 敏感設定走環境變數 → 不 hardcode
41	
42	**2. RBAC 三層權限** (`user` / `admin` / `super_admin`)，完整矩陣：
43	
44	| 動作 | user | admin | super_admin |
45	|------|:---:|:---:|:---:|
46	| 登入登出、改自己密碼 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
47	| LLM 對話、多 session | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
48	| 查看/刪除自己的對話 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
49	| 新增使用者 (role=user) | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
50	| 新增使用者 (role=admin) | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
51	| 列出所有使用者 | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
52	| 啟用/停用 user 帳號 | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
53	| 啟用/停用 admin 帳號 | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
54	| 將 user 升為 admin | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
55	| 匯出所有人對話 (JSON) | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
56	
57	**系統不變式 (必須保證)**
58	- **(I-1) super_admin 首次啟動自動建立**：從環境變數讀帳密，首啟動建立；重複啟動 idempotent (已存在不重建、不報錯)；環境變數缺失時明確報錯而非靜默啟動成無管理員。**禁止 hardcode 帳密**。
59	- **(I-2) 任何時刻至少 1 個 active super_admin**：需在 README 說明如何保證 (見 §3.4 設計)。
60	
61	**3. LLM 對話功能**
62	- 每使用者多個對話 session (ChatGPT 式左側列表)
63	- 對話內容持久化
64	- **LLM 回應逐字串流顯示 (streaming)**
65	- session 切換時正確載入歷史
66	- 使用者可刪除自己的對話
67	
68	**4. 管理功能**
69	- 後端 Admin / Super Admin API **必須完整實作**
70	- 前端 admin 頁非必須 (可用 `/docs`、curl 演示)；做了算 Bonus
71	
72	**5. Super Admin 匯出**：匯出所有使用者對話，格式 JSON
73	
74	**6. API 文件**：維護 apiflask 內建 `/docs`，schema / tag / description 完整準確
75	
76	**7. 測試**：對 auth、RBAC、對話、匯出 等核心邏輯撰寫測試 (類型/覆蓋自行判斷)
77	
78	**8. 部署**：Dockerfile + docker-compose；`docker compose up` 一鍵起 (含 DB)；`.env.example`；README 註明預設管理員 / seed；說明 production worker 選擇理由
79	
80	### 1.3 Bonus (選做，本計畫挑做)
81	
82	挑選原則：高工程訊號、低相對成本、與 Core 共用基礎設施。
83	
84	| 取捨 | Bonus 項目 | 為什麼挑 / 不挑 |
85	|------|-----------|----------------|
86	| ✅ 做 | Structured logging + Request ID tracking | 幾乎零成本，直接展現 observability 思維 |
87	| ✅ 做 | 完整 health check (DB + LLM 上游) | compose healthcheck 會用到，順手做 |
88	| ✅ 做 | 前端 Admin / Super Admin 頁 | 明列 Bonus，且能讓 demo 錄影更完整 |
89	| ✅ 做 | 對話標題自動生成 | UX 亮點，串接 LLM 時順帶實作，成本低 |
90	| 🟡 有餘力 | Rate limiting、graceful shutdown、Markdown/code highlight、dark mode | Core 全綠後才碰 |
91	| ❌ 不做 | E2E (Playwright)、CI pipeline、refresh token rotation、metrics endpoint | 時間風險高，邊際效益遞減；在 README「未完成項目」誠實說明取捨理由 |
92	
93	### 1.4 交付物清單 (繳交前 checklist)
94	
95	1. Git repo (含 `.git`，commit history 要乾淨、能對應 transcript)
96	2. `README.md`：啟動步驟、預設帳密/seed、**Tech Choices & Rationale**、已完成 Bonus、已知限制/未完成項目
97	3. 完整 LLM 對話 **原始 JSONL** transcript (不接受事後整理的 Markdown)
98	4. 3–5 分鐘螢幕錄影 (登入 / 對話 / admin 操作 / 匯出；可用 `/docs`)
99	5. `TOOLING.md` (用作業提供的範本，逐節填寫，無此項填「無」)
100	6. `REDACTION.md` (說明遮蔽了哪些類型敏感資訊)
101	
102	> ⚠️ 易漏點：**transcript 與 REDACTION 是評分項**。從 Day 1 就保留 `~/.claude/projects/<...>/` 對話紀錄，繳交前統一 redact API key / secret / 私人路徑，撤換任何不慎外洩的憑證。
103	
104	---
105	
106	## 2. 技術選型與 Trade-offs (README 的 Tech Choices 段落雛形)
107	
108	**資料庫 — PostgreSQL**
109	- 選它：production-grade、SQLAlchemy + Alembic 生態成熟、原生支援 JSONB (存 message metadata 方便)、可展現連線池 / 索引 / 交易範圍等工程考量。
110	- Trade-off：相較 SQLite 多一個 service，但 compose 已涵蓋；一鍵起仍成立。
111	
112	**LLM Provider — Groq**
113	- 選它：free tier、OpenAI 相容 API (可直接用 `openai` SDK)、原生 SSE streaming、延遲低。
114	- Trade-off：模型清單受限於 Groq 供應，但作業明說 provider/價格不計分。用 OpenAI 相容介面包一層 adapter，未來換 provider 只改設定。
115	
116	**JWT 演算法 — HS256**
117	- 單體服務、單一簽發者，HS256 + 環境變數 secret 足夠且最簡。
118	- Trade-off：若要多服務驗證會選 RS256；本作業不需要，避免 over-engineering。README 註明此判斷。
119	
120	**密碼雜湊 — Argon2id**
121	- 現代首選 (記憶體硬、抗 GPU)。bcrypt 為可接受備案。
122	
123	**Production worker — Gunicorn + Uvicorn worker / 或 gevent**
124	- apiflask 是 WSGI。streaming (SSE) 需要能長連線、不被 worker 提前回收。
125	- 決策：`gunicorn` 搭 `gevent` worker (對 SSE 長連線與 I/O 密集的 LLM 轉發友善)；worker 數 `2*CPU+1` 起步。README 說明：選 gevent 是因為 SSE 是長時 I/O 等待，thread/sync worker 容易吃滿；理由寫清楚。
126	
127	**Container 策略**
128	- 後端多階段 build (builder 裝依賴 → slim runtime)，non-root user，`.dockerignore` 控制 context。
129	- 前端 build 出靜態檔，由 nginx 或 Vite preview 服務；compose 內 `depends_on` + healthcheck 控制啟動順序。
130	
131	---
132	
133	## 3. 架構設計 (Clean Architecture / DDD 視角)
134	
135	### 3.1 分層
136	
137	```
138	app/
139	  domain/            # 純業務：entity、value object、領域規則、Port (interface)
140	    user.py          #   User aggregate、Role、RBAC policy
141	    chat.py          #   ChatSession、Message aggregate
142	    ports.py         #   UserRepository / ChatRepository / LLMProvider (抽象)
143	  application/        # use case / service layer：編排，不含框架
144	    auth_service.py
145	    user_admin_service.py
146	    chat_service.py
147	    export_service.py
148	  infrastructure/     # 框架/IO 實作
149	    db/              #   SQLAlchemy models、repository 實作、Alembic
150	    llm/             #   Groq adapter (實作 LLMProvider port)
151	    security/        #   JWT、Argon2 雜湊
152	    config.py        #   Pydantic Settings (讀環境變數)
153	    seed.py          #   super_admin idempotent seed
154	  interface/          # apiflask blueprint、schema、權限 decorator
155	    api/auth.py
156	    api/chat.py
157	    api/admin.py
158	    api/health.py
159	    schemas.py       #   marshmallow / apispec request/response
160	    deps.py          #   DI 組裝 (request scope)
161	```
162	
163	- **依賴方向**：interface → application → domain；infrastructure 實作 domain 定義的 port。domain 不 import 任何框架。
164	- **DI**：在 `interface/deps.py` 用工廠把 repository、LLMProvider、service 組起來注入 view，方便測試替身 (fake repo / fake LLM)。
165	- **避免**：business logic 落在 schema/view、fat model、signals 魔法行為。
166	
167	### 3.2 資料模型 (PostgreSQL)
168	
169	```
170	users
171	  id (uuid pk)
172	  username (unique, citext/lower)
173	  password_hash (argon2)
174	  role (enum: user|admin|super_admin)
175	  is_active (bool)
176	  created_at, updated_at
177	
178	chat_sessions
179	  id (uuid pk)
180	  user_id (fk users, on delete cascade)
181	  title (text, nullable → 自動生成)
182	  created_at, updated_at
183	  index (user_id, updated_at desc)   # 左側列表排序
184	
185	messages
186	  id (uuid pk)
187	  session_id (fk chat_sessions, cascade)
188	  role (enum: user|assistant|system)
189	  content (text)
190	  created_at
191	  index (session_id, created_at)     # 載入歷史、避免 N+1
192	```
193	
194	- 列出 session 列表 + 訊息時注意 **N+1**：用 `selectinload` 或分查詢；列表頁不要 eager load 全部 messages。
195	- 匯出時用 streaming query / 分批，避免一次撈爆記憶體 (資料量大時)。
196	
197	### 3.3 RBAC 設計
198	
199	- 用**單一權限矩陣** (枚舉 `Permission` → 允許的 role 集合) 集中定義，view 上掛 `@require_permission(Permission.X)` decorator。
200	- 權限檢查走 application service / decorator，**不散落在 view body**。
201	- 跨角色約束 (例：admin 不能停用 admin、不能建 admin) 在 service 層用領域規則擋，並回 403 而非 500。
202	
203	### 3.4 系統不變式的工程保證
204	
205	**(I-1) super_admin idempotent seed**
206	- 啟動時 `seed.py` 讀 `SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME` / `SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD`：
207	  - 缺任一 → **明確 raise，啟動失敗** (fail fast，不靜默成無管理員)。
208	  - 已存在同名 super_admin → 跳過 (idempotent)，不重建、不報錯。
209	  - 不存在 → 建立。
210	- 用 `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` 或先查再建 + 唯一約束，避免並發重複建立。
211	- 在 entrypoint：先跑 Alembic migration，再跑 seed，再啟 worker。
212	
213	**(I-2) 至少 1 個 active super_admin**
214	- 設計決策 (擇一，寫進 README)：**不提供 super_admin 自我退場 / 自我降級 / 自我停用**。
215	  - 任何「停用 / 降級 super_admin」操作前，在 service 層檢查「目前 active super_admin 數量 > 1」，否則拒絕 (409/422)。
216	  - 加上 seed 保證系統永遠有初始 super_admin → 結合矩陣限制，理論上不可能進入「無超管」狀態。
217	- README 用一段話說明此保證 (作業明確要求)。
218	
219	### 3.5 LLM Streaming 設計 (最高風險，重點)
220	
221	- **傳輸**：SSE (`text/event-stream`)。比 WebSocket 簡單、單向夠用、apiflask/WSGI + gevent 可長連線。
222	- **流程**：
223	  1. 前端送出 user message → 後端先持久化 user message。
224	  2. 後端呼叫 Groq (`stream=True`)，逐 token `yield` SSE event 給前端。
225	  3. 串流結束後，把完整 assistant 回應**一次性持久化** (避免每 token 寫 DB)。
226	  4. 斷流 / 上游錯誤：持久化「已收到的部分內容 + 錯誤標記」，前端顯示重試。
227	- **gotcha**：
228	  - apiflask response schema 對 streaming 端點不適用，需用裸 `Response(stream_with_context(...))`，並在 OpenAPI 手動註明此端點行為。
229	  - gunicorn 要用 gevent worker，且關閉 response buffering；nginx 前置時要關 `proxy_buffering`。
230	  - JWT 驗證要在開始串流前完成 (header 帶 token)。
231	
232	---
233	
234	## 4. 建構順序與依賴 (為什麼這樣排)
235	
236	```
237	[地基] Docker+DB+migration+設定   ← 一切的前提
238	   │
239	[Auth] 註冊由 admin 建/登入登出/改密碼/JWT
240	   │
241	[RBAC] 權限矩陣 + decorator + admin API
242	   │
243	[Chat 持久化] session/message CRUD + 歷史載入
244	   │
245	[LLM Streaming] 接 Groq + SSE      ← 風險最高，依賴上面全部
246	   │
247	[匯出 + /docs + 測試 + Bonus]
248	```
249	
250	刻意把 **LLM streaming 放最後**：它依賴 auth (誰在用)、chat model (存哪)、worker 設定 (gevent)。前面不穩就接 streaming 會反覆返工。先用 mock LLM provider 打通 chat CRUD 與前端，最後才換真 Groq。
251	
252	---
253	
254	## 5. 7 天分階段計畫
255	
256	> 每天結束都應有「可 demo 的增量」。每完成一塊就 commit，訊息清楚 (利於 transcript 對照)。
257	
258	### Day 1 — 地基與骨架 (環境穩，後面才快)
259	- 初始化 git repo、monorepo 結構 (`backend/`、`frontend/`、`docker/`)。
260	- 後端 apiflask skeleton + Pydantic Settings 讀環境變數 + `/docs` 可開。
261	- PostgreSQL 接上、SQLAlchemy + Alembic 初始 migration (users / chat_sessions / messages)。
262	- Dockerfile (多階段) + docker-compose (api + db) + `.env.example`。
263	- **驗收**：`docker compose up` 起得來，`/docs` 打得開，migration 自動跑。
264	- **產出**：可一鍵起的空殼。
265	
266	### Day 2 — Auth 與 super_admin seed (不變式 I-1)
267	- Argon2 密碼雜湊、JWT 簽發/驗證 (HS256, secret 走 env)。
268	- 登入 / 登出 / 改自己密碼 endpoint + schema。
269	- **super_admin idempotent seed** + 環境變數缺失 fail-fast。
270	- 對 auth + seed 寫測試 (含「缺 env 報錯」「重複啟動不重建」案例)。
271	- **驗收**：compose 起來後能直接用 env 設定的 super_admin 登入；重啟不爆。
272	
273	### Day 3 — RBAC 與 Admin / Super Admin API (不變式 I-2)
274	- 權限矩陣 + `@require_permission` decorator。
275	- 完整 admin API：建 user/admin、列出使用者、啟用/停用、升級 user→admin、跨角色約束。
276	- 「至少 1 個 active super_admin」service 層守門。
277	- RBAC 測試：每個矩陣格子至少一個 allow/deny 案例。
278	- **驗收**：用 `/docs` 或 curl 走完整管理流程，越權回 403。
279	
280	### Day 4 — Chat 持久化 (先用 mock LLM)
281	- chat session CRUD (多 session、列表、切換載入歷史、刪除)。
282	- message 持久化、N+1 檢查、索引。
283	- 先接 **mock LLM provider** (回固定 / echo)，把前後端 chat 流程打通。
284	- **驗收**：能開多個對話、切換載入正確、刪除生效；資料持久化。
285	
286	### Day 5 — 真・LLM Streaming (最高風險日，留緩衝)
287	- Groq adapter 實作 `LLMProvider` port (OpenAI 相容、`stream=True`)。
288	- SSE 端點 + 前端逐字渲染 + 串流結束後持久化 assistant 回應。
289	- gunicorn + gevent worker 設定；斷流 / 上游錯誤處理。
290	- **Bonus**：對話標題自動生成 (首則訊息後用 LLM 產標題)。
291	- **驗收**：前端看到逐字串流；重整後歷史完整；上游錯誤不會壞掉 session。
292	
293	### Day 6 — 匯出、前端整合、Bonus 基礎設施
294	- Super Admin 匯出所有對話 (JSON，分批查詢)。
295	- **Bonus**：前端 Admin / Super Admin 管理頁 (對應後端端點)。
296	- **Bonus**：structured logging + request ID middleware；health check (DB + Groq 上游)；compose healthcheck 串起來。
297	- 補測試覆蓋 (匯出、邊界案例)。
298	- **驗收**：匯出 JSON 結構正確；admin 頁可操作；`/health` 反映 DB+LLM 狀態。
299	
300	### Day 7 — 收斂、文件、交付物
301	- README：啟動步驟、預設帳密/seed、**Tech Choices & Rationale**、已完成 Bonus、**已知限制/未完成項目** (誠實寫沒做的 Bonus 與理由)。
302	- `TOOLING.md` (照範本逐節)、`REDACTION.md`。
303	- 整理並 **redact transcript** (JSONL)，撤換任何外洩憑證。
304	- 錄 3–5 分鐘 demo (登入 / 對話 streaming / admin 操作 / 匯出)。
305	- 全量跑測試、`docker compose up` 從乾淨環境驗一次一鍵起。
306	- 打包 zip (含 `.git`)。
307	- **驗收**：別人拿到 zip，照 README 能一鍵跑起來。
308	
309	> **緩衝原則**：若進度落後，Day 5 的 streaming 與 Day 7 的交付物優先；Day 6 的 Bonus 全部可砍，砍掉就在 README 未完成項目誠實說明。
310	
311	---
312	
313	## 6. 測試策略
314	
315	- **單元 (pytest)**：domain 規則 (RBAC 矩陣、不變式守門)、密碼雜湊、JWT。用 fake repository，不碰 DB。
316	- **整合**：service + 真 DB (pytest fixture 起測試 schema / 交易回滾)、auth flow、admin flow、匯出。
317	- **LLM**：用 fake `LLMProvider` 注入，測 streaming 端點的事件序列與持久化，不打真 API (穩定、免費、可重現)。
318	- **factory_boy** 造測試資料。
319	- 最低標：作業點名的 auth / RBAC / 對話 / 匯出 四塊都有測試。
320	
321	---
322	
323	## 7. 風險清單與對策
324	
325	| 風險 | 影響 | 對策 |
326	|------|------|------|
327	| SSE streaming + WSGI worker 設定踩坑 | 高 | Day 4 先用 mock 打通流程，Day 5 才換真 Groq；預留緩衝 |
328	| super_admin 不變式設計沒講清楚 | 中 (扣分點) | §3.4 決策 + README 明確說明保證機制 |
329	| transcript 沒留 / 沒 redact | 中 (繳交瑕疵) | Day 1 就確認紀錄路徑；繳交前統一 redact + REDACTION.md |
330	| Bonus 吃掉 Core 時間 | 中 | Core 全綠才碰 Bonus；Day 6 Bonus 可全砍 |
331	| N+1 / 匯出記憶體爆 | 低中 | 索引 + selectinload；匯出分批 |
332	| 密碼 / secret hardcode | 高 (硬傷) | 全走 Pydantic Settings + `.env`；`.dockerignore` 排除 `.env` |
333	
334	---
335	
336	## 8. 立即可動的第一步 (Day 1 開工順序)
337	
338	1. `git init`，建 monorepo 目錄與 `.gitignore` / `.dockerignore` / `.env.example`。
339	2. 後端 apiflask 最小 app + Pydantic Settings + `/health` + `/docs`。
340	3. compose 起 PostgreSQL，SQLAlchemy 連上，Alembic 初始 migration。
341	4. 寫 Dockerfile (多階段) + compose，確認 `docker compose up` 一鍵起。
342	5. 第一個有意義的 commit：「chore: project skeleton, docker compose one-command up」。
343	
344	> 地基穩了，Day 2 之後每天都是疊加可 demo 的功能，而不是和環境奮鬥。
345

file.content:
# LLM Chat Web 應用 — 開發計畫 (PLAN.md)

> 面試作業：多人 LLM Chat Web 應用。本文件整理需求、定案技術選型、架構分層，並排出 7 天可落地的分階段開發計畫。

---

## 0. 結論先行 (TL;DR)

- **選型定案**：apiflask (後端) + Vue 3 (前端) + **PostgreSQL** + **Groq** (OpenAI 相容、free tier、原生 streaming) + Docker Compose 一鍵起。
- **最關鍵的工程判斷點不是「功能」，而是四件事**：
  1. `super_admin` 的 **idempotent seed** + 「至少 1 個 active super_admin」不變式。
  2. **streaming** 端到端打通 (SSE，含 DB 持久化的時機與斷流處理)。
  3. **RBAC** 用一個集中的權限矩陣驅動，避免散落在各 view。
  4. **JWT secret / API key** 全走環境變數，零 hardcode，`.env.example` 完整。
- **建構順序**：先打地基 (Docker + DB + migration + 設定管理) → 再 auth/JWT → 再 RBAC → 再 chat 持久化 → **最後才接 streaming 與 LLM** (風險最高、最依賴前面)。LLM 串接放最後是刻意的：它依賴 session/message model 與 auth 都先穩。
- **範圍策略**：Core 全綠且品質穩定為達標線。Bonus 只挑 4 項高 CP 值且能展現工程深度的：structured logging + request ID、health check (DB+LLM)、前端 admin 頁、對話標題自動生成。
- **每個 commit 都要能對應到 transcript**：因為評分會交叉比對 git history 與 AI 對話紀錄，務必小步提交、訊息清楚。

---

## 1. 需求整理

### 1.1 強制技術棧

| 角色 | 指定技術 | 本計畫定案 |
|------|---------|-----------|
| 後端 | apiflask | apiflask (Flask + marshmallow/apispec，內建 `/docs`) |
| 前端 | Vue 3 | Vue 3 + Vite + TypeScript + Pinia |
| 容器化 | Docker + docker-compose | 多階段 Dockerfile + compose (api / db / frontend) |
| 資料庫 | SQLite / PostgreSQL / MariaDB / MongoDB 擇一 | **PostgreSQL 16** |
| LLM 介接 | 任一線上服務 (free tier 佳) | **Groq** (OpenAI 相容 SDK、原生 SSE streaming) |

### 1.2 Core 功能需求 (必做，全綠才達標)

**1. 認證與帳號**
- 帳號密碼登入 / 登出
- JWT 做 session 管理
- 使用者可改自己的密碼
- 密碼用合適方式儲存 → **Argon2id** (或 bcrypt)，絕不明文
- JWT secret / 敏感設定走環境變數 → 不 hardcode

**2. RBAC 三層權限** (`user` / `admin` / `super_admin`)，完整矩陣：

| 動作 | user | admin | super_admin |
|------|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 登入登出、改自己密碼 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| LLM 對話、多 session | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| 查看/刪除自己的對話 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| 新增使用者 (role=user) | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| 新增使用者 (role=admin) | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
| 列出所有使用者 | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| 啟用/停用 user 帳號 | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| 啟用/停用 admin 帳號 | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
| 將 user 升為 admin | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |
| 匯出所有人對話 (JSON) | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ |

**系統不變式 (必須保證)**
- **(I-1) super_admin 首次啟動自動建立**：從環境變數讀帳密，首啟動建立；重複啟動 idempotent (已存在不重建、不報錯)；環境變數缺失時明確報錯而非靜默啟動成無管理員。**禁止 hardcode 帳密**。
- **(I-2) 任何時刻至少 1 個 active super_admin**：需在 README 說明如何保證 (見 §3.4 設計)。

**3. LLM 對話功能**
- 每使用者多個對話 session (ChatGPT 式左側列表)
- 對話內容持久化
- **LLM 回應逐字串流顯示 (streaming)**
- session 切換時正確載入歷史
- 使用者可刪除自己的對話

**4. 管理功能**
- 後端 Admin / Super Admin API **必須完整實作**
- 前端 admin 頁非必須 (可用 `/docs`、curl 演示)；做了算 Bonus

**5. Super Admin 匯出**：匯出所有使用者對話，格式 JSON

**6. API 文件**：維護 apiflask 內建 `/docs`，schema / tag / description 完整準確

**7. 測試**：對 auth、RBAC、對話、匯出 等核心邏輯撰寫測試 (類型/覆蓋自行判斷)

**8. 部署**：Dockerfile + docker-compose；`docker compose up` 一鍵起 (含 DB)；`.env.example`；README 註明預設管理員 / seed；說明 production worker 選擇理由

### 1.3 Bonus (選做，本計畫挑做)

挑選原則：高工程訊號、低相對成本、與 Core 共用基礎設施。

| 取捨 | Bonus 項目 | 為什麼挑 / 不挑 |
|------|-----------|----------------|
| ✅ 做 | Structured logging + Request ID tracking | 幾乎零成本，直接展現 observability 思維 |
| ✅ 做 | 完整 health check (DB + LLM 上游) | compose healthcheck 會用到，順手做 |
| ✅ 做 | 前端 Admin / Super Admin 頁 | 明列 Bonus，且能讓 demo 錄影更完整 |
| ✅ 做 | 對話標題自動生成 | UX 亮點，串接 LLM 時順帶實作，成本低 |
| 🟡 有餘力 | Rate limiting、graceful shutdown、Markdown/code highlight、dark mode | Core 全綠後才碰 |
| ❌ 不做 | E2E (Playwright)、CI pipeline、refresh token rotation、metrics endpoint | 時間風險高，邊際效益遞減；在 README「未完成項目」誠實說明取捨理由 |

### 1.4 交付物清單 (繳交前 checklist)

1. Git repo (含 `.git`，commit history 要乾淨、能對應 transcript)
2. `README.md`：啟動步驟、預設帳密/seed、**Tech Choices & Rationale**、已完成 Bonus、已知限制/未完成項目
3. 完整 LLM 對話 **原始 JSONL** transcript (不接受事後整理的 Markdown)
4. 3–5 分鐘螢幕錄影 (登入 / 對話 / admin 操作 / 匯出；可用 `/docs`)
5. `TOOLING.md` (用作業提供的範本，逐節填寫，無此項填「無」)
6. `REDACTION.md` (說明遮蔽了哪些類型敏感資訊)

> ⚠️ 易漏點：**transcript 與 REDACTION 是評分項**。從 Day 1 就保留 `~/.claude/projects/<...>/` 對話紀錄，繳交前統一 redact API key / secret / 私人路徑，撤換任何不慎外洩的憑證。

---

## 2. 技術選型與 Trade-offs (README 的 Tech Choices 段落雛形)

**資料庫 — PostgreSQL**
- 選它：production-grade、SQLAlchemy + Alembic 生態成熟、原生支援 JSONB (存 message metadata 方便)、可展現連線池 / 索引 / 交易範圍等工程考量。
- Trade-off：相較 SQLite 多一個 service，但 compose 已涵蓋；一鍵起仍成立。

**LLM Provider — Groq**
- 選它：free tier、OpenAI 相容 API (可直接用 `openai` SDK)、原生 SSE streaming、延遲低。
- Trade-off：模型清單受限於 Groq 供應，但作業明說 provider/價格不計分。用 OpenAI 相容介面包一層 adapter，未來換 provider 只改設定。

**JWT 演算法 — HS256**
- 單體服務、單一簽發者，HS256 + 環境變數 secret 足夠且最簡。
- Trade-off：若要多服務驗證會選 RS256；本作業不需要，避免 over-engineering。README 註明此判斷。

**密碼雜湊 — Argon2id**
- 現代首選 (記憶體硬、抗 GPU)。bcrypt 為可接受備案。

**Production worker — Gunicorn + Uvicorn worker / 或 gevent**
- apiflask 是 WSGI。streaming (SSE) 需要能長連線、不被 worker 提前回收。
- 決策：`gunicorn` 搭 `gevent` worker (對 SSE 長連線與 I/O 密集的 LLM 轉發友善)；worker 數 `2*CPU+1` 起步。README 說明：選 gevent 是因為 SSE 是長時 I/O 等待，thread/sync worker 容易吃滿；理由寫清楚。

**Container 策略**
- 後端多階段 build (builder 裝依賴 → slim runtime)，non-root user，`.dockerignore` 控制 context。
- 前端 build 出靜態檔，由 nginx 或 Vite preview 服務；compose 內 `depends_on` + healthcheck 控制啟動順序。

---

## 3. 架構設計 (Clean Architecture / DDD 視角)

### 3.1 分層

```
app/
  domain/            # 純業務：entity、value object、領域規則、Port (interface)
    user.py          #   User aggregate、Role、RBAC policy
    chat.py          #   ChatSession、Message aggregate
    ports.py         #   UserRepository / ChatRepository / LLMProvider (抽象)
  application/        # use case / service layer：編排，不含框架
    auth_service.py
    user_admin_service.py
    chat_service.py
    export_service.py
  infrastructure/     # 框架/IO 實作
    db/              #   SQLAlchemy models、repository 實作、Alembic
    llm/             #   Groq adapter (實作 LLMProvider port)
    security/        #   JWT、Argon2 雜湊
    config.py        #   Pydantic Settings (讀環境變數)
    seed.py          #   super_admin idempotent seed
  interface/          # apiflask blueprint、schema、權限 decorator
    api/auth.py
    api/chat.py
    api/admin.py
    api/health.py
    schemas.py       #   marshmallow / apispec request/response
    deps.py          #   DI 組裝 (request scope)
```

- **依賴方向**：interface → application → domain；infrastructure 實作 domain 定義的 port。domain 不 import 任何框架。
- **DI**：在 `interface/deps.py` 用工廠把 repository、LLMProvider、service 組起來注入 view，方便測試替身 (fake repo / fake LLM)。
- **避免**：business logic 落在 schema/view、fat model、signals 魔法行為。

### 3.2 資料模型 (PostgreSQL)

```
users
  id (uuid pk)
  username (unique, citext/lower)
  password_hash (argon2)
  role (enum: user|admin|super_admin)
  is_active (bool)
  created_at, updated_at

chat_sessions
  id (uuid pk)
  user_id (fk users, on delete cascade)
  title (text, nullable → 自動生成)
  created_at, updated_at
  index (user_id, updated_at desc)   # 左側列表排序

messages
  id (uuid pk)
  session_id (fk chat_sessions, cascade)
  role (enum: user|assistant|system)
  content (text)
  created_at
  index (session_id, created_at)     # 載入歷史、避免 N+1
```

- 列出 session 列表 + 訊息時注意 **N+1**：用 `selectinload` 或分查詢；列表頁不要 eager load 全部 messages。
- 匯出時用 streaming query / 分批，避免一次撈爆記憶體 (資料量大時)。

### 3.3 RBAC 設計

- 用**單一權限矩陣** (枚舉 `Permission` → 允許的 role 集合) 集中定義，view 上掛 `@require_permission(Permission.X)` decorator。
- 權限檢查走 application service / decorator，**不散落在 view body**。
- 跨角色約束 (例：admin 不能停用 admin、不能建 admin) 在 service 層用領域規則擋，並回 403 而非 500。

### 3.4 系統不變式的工程保證

**(I-1) super_admin idempotent seed**
- 啟動時 `seed.py` 讀 `SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME` / `SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD`：
  - 缺任一 → **明確 raise，啟動失敗** (fail fast，不靜默成無管理員)。
  - 已存在同名 super_admin → 跳過 (idempotent)，不重建、不報錯。
  - 不存在 → 建立。
- 用 `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` 或先查再建 + 唯一約束，避免並發重複建立。
- 在 entrypoint：先跑 Alembic migration，再跑 seed，再啟 worker。

**(I-2) 至少 1 個 active super_admin**
- 設計決策 (擇一，寫進 README)：**不提供 super_admin 自我退場 / 自我降級 / 自我停用**。
  - 任何「停用 / 降級 super_admin」操作前，在 service 層檢查「目前 active super_admin 數量 > 1」，否則拒絕 (409/422)。
  - 加上 seed 保證系統永遠有初始 super_admin → 結合矩陣限制，理論上不可能進入「無超管」狀態。
- README 用一段話說明此保證 (作業明確要求)。

### 3.5 LLM Streaming 設計 (最高風險，重點)

- **傳輸**：SSE (`text/event-stream`)。比 WebSocket 簡單、單向夠用、apiflask/WSGI + gevent 可長連線。
- **流程**：
  1. 前端送出 user message → 後端先持久化 user message。
  2. 後端呼叫 Groq (`stream=True`)，逐 token `yield` SSE event 給前端。
  3. 串流結束後，把完整 assistant 回應**一次性持久化** (避免每 token 寫 DB)。
  4. 斷流 / 上游錯誤：持久化「已收到的部分內容 + 錯誤標記」，前端顯示重試。
- **gotcha**：
  - apiflask response schema 對 streaming 端點不適用，需用裸 `Response(stream_with_context(...))`，並在 OpenAPI 手動註明此端點行為。
  - gunicorn 要用 gevent worker，且關閉 response buffering；nginx 前置時要關 `proxy_buffering`。
  - JWT 驗證要在開始串流前完成 (header 帶 token)。

---

## 4. 建構順序與依賴 (為什麼這樣排)

```
[地基] Docker+DB+migration+設定   ← 一切的前提
   │
[Auth] 註冊由 admin 建/登入登出/改密碼/JWT
   │
[RBAC] 權限矩陣 + decorator + admin API
   │
[Chat 持久化] session/message CRUD + 歷史載入
   │
[LLM Streaming] 接 Groq + SSE      ← 風險最高，依賴上面全部
   │
[匯出 + /docs + 測試 + Bonus]
```

刻意把 **LLM streaming 放最後**：它依賴 auth (誰在用)、chat model (存哪)、worker 設定 (gevent)。前面不穩就接 streaming 會反覆返工。先用 mock LLM provider 打通 chat CRUD 與前端，最後才換真 Groq。

---

## 5. 7 天分階段計畫

> 每天結束都應有「可 demo 的增量」。每完成一塊就 commit，訊息清楚 (利於 transcript 對照)。

### Day 1 — 地基與骨架 (環境穩，後面才快)
- 初始化 git repo、monorepo 結構 (`backend/`、`frontend/`、`docker/`)。
- 後端 apiflask skeleton + Pydantic Settings 讀環境變數 + `/docs` 可開。
- PostgreSQL 接上、SQLAlchemy + Alembic 初始 migration (users / chat_sessions / messages)。
- Dockerfile (多階段) + docker-compose (api + db) + `.env.example`。
- **驗收**：`docker compose up` 起得來，`/docs` 打得開，migration 自動跑。
- **產出**：可一鍵起的空殼。

### Day 2 — Auth 與 super_admin seed (不變式 I-1)
- Argon2 密碼雜湊、JWT 簽發/驗證 (HS256, secret 走 env)。
- 登入 / 登出 / 改自己密碼 endpoint + schema。
- **super_admin idempotent seed** + 環境變數缺失 fail-fast。
- 對 auth + seed 寫測試 (含「缺 env 報錯」「重複啟動不重建」案例)。
- **驗收**：compose 起來後能直接用 env 設定的 super_admin 登入；重啟不爆。

### Day 3 — RBAC 與 Admin / Super Admin API (不變式 I-2)
- 權限矩陣 + `@require_permission` decorator。
- 完整 admin API：建 user/admin、列出使用者、啟用/停用、升級 user→admin、跨角色約束。
- 「至少 1 個 active super_admin」service 層守門。
- RBAC 測試：每個矩陣格子至少一個 allow/deny 案例。
- **驗收**：用 `/docs` 或 curl 走完整管理流程，越權回 403。

### Day 4 — Chat 持久化 (先用 mock LLM)
- chat session CRUD (多 session、列表、切換載入歷史、刪除)。
- message 持久化、N+1 檢查、索引。
- 先接 **mock LLM provider** (回固定 / echo)，把前後端 chat 流程打通。
- **驗收**：能開多個對話、切換載入正確、刪除生效；資料持久化。

### Day 5 — 真・LLM Streaming (最高風險日，留緩衝)
- Groq adapter 實作 `LLMProvider` port (OpenAI 相容、`stream=True`)。
- SSE 端點 + 前端逐字渲染 + 串流結束後持久化 assistant 回應。
- gunicorn + gevent worker 設定；斷流 / 上游錯誤處理。
- **Bonus**：對話標題自動生成 (首則訊息後用 LLM 產標題)。
- **驗收**：前端看到逐字串流；重整後歷史完整；上游錯誤不會壞掉 session。

### Day 6 — 匯出、前端整合、Bonus 基礎設施
- Super Admin 匯出所有對話 (JSON，分批查詢)。
- **Bonus**：前端 Admin / Super Admin 管理頁 (對應後端端點)。
- **Bonus**：structured logging + request ID middleware；health check (DB + Groq 上游)；compose healthcheck 串起來。
- 補測試覆蓋 (匯出、邊界案例)。
- **驗收**：匯出 JSON 結構正確；admin 頁可操作；`/health` 反映 DB+LLM 狀態。

### Day 7 — 收斂、文件、交付物
- README：啟動步驟、預設帳密/seed、**Tech Choices & Rationale**、已完成 Bonus、**已知限制/未完成項目** (誠實寫沒做的 Bonus 與理由)。
- `TOOLING.md` (照範本逐節)、`REDACTION.md`。
- 整理並 **redact transcript** (JSONL)，撤換任何外洩憑證。
- 錄 3–5 分鐘 demo (登入 / 對話 streaming / admin 操作 / 匯出)。
- 全量跑測試、`docker compose up` 從乾淨環境驗一次一鍵起。
- 打包 zip (含 `.git`)。
- **驗收**：別人拿到 zip，照 README 能一鍵跑起來。

> **緩衝原則**：若進度落後，Day 5 的 streaming 與 Day 7 的交付物優先；Day 6 的 Bonus 全部可砍，砍掉就在 README 未完成項目誠實說明。

---

## 6. 測試策略

- **單元 (pytest)**：domain 規則 (RBAC 矩陣、不變式守門)、密碼雜湊、JWT。用 fake repository，不碰 DB。
- **整合**：service + 真 DB (pytest fixture 起測試 schema / 交易回滾)、auth flow、admin flow、匯出。
- **LLM**：用 fake `LLMProvider` 注入，測 streaming 端點的事件序列與持久化，不打真 API (穩定、免費、可重現)。
- **factory_boy** 造測試資料。
- 最低標：作業點名的 auth / RBAC / 對話 / 匯出 四塊都有測試。

---

## 7. 風險清單與對策

| 風險 | 影響 | 對策 |
|------|------|------|
| SSE streaming + WSGI worker 設定踩坑 | 高 | Day 4 先用 mock 打通流程，Day 5 才換真 Groq；預留緩衝 |
| super_admin 不變式設計沒講清楚 | 中 (扣分點) | §3.4 決策 + README 明確說明保證機制 |
| transcript 沒留 / 沒 redact | 中 (繳交瑕疵) | Day 1 就確認紀錄路徑；繳交前統一 redact + REDACTION.md |
| Bonus 吃掉 Core 時間 | 中 | Core 全綠才碰 Bonus；Day 6 Bonus 可全砍 |
| N+1 / 匯出記憶體爆 | 低中 | 索引 + selectinload；匯出分批 |
| 密碼 / secret hardcode | 高 (硬傷) | 全走 Pydantic Settings + `.env`；`.dockerignore` 排除 `.env` |

---

## 8. 立即可動的第一步 (Day 1 開工順序)

1. `git init`，建 monorepo 目錄與 `.gitignore` / `.dockerignore` / `.env.example`。
2. 後端 apiflask 最小 app + Pydantic Settings + `/health` + `/docs`。
3. compose 起 PostgreSQL，SQLAlchemy 連上，Alembic 初始 migration。
4. 寫 Dockerfile (多階段) + compose，確認 `docker compose up` 一鍵起。
5. 第一個有意義的 commit：「chore: project skeleton, docker compose one-command up」。

> 地基穩了，Day 2 之後每天都是疊加可 demo 的功能，而不是和環境奮鬥。

20	@auth_bp.input(LoginIn)
21	@auth_bp.output(TokenOut)
22	@auth_bp.doc(summary="Log in", description="Exchange username/password for a JWT access token.")
23	def login(json_data: dict):
24	    user = auth_service().authenticate(json_data["username"], json_data["password"])
25	    get_db().commit()  # persist any transparent password rehash
26	    token = create_access_token(user.id, user.role)
27	    return {"access_token": token, "token_type": "bearer"}
28	
29	
30	@auth_bp.post("/logout")
31	@auth_bp.output(MessageOut)
32	@auth_bp.auth_required(auth)
33	@auth_bp.doc(
34	    summary="Log out",
35	    description="Stateless JWT: the server has no session to destroy. The client "
36	    "discards the token. Returned for symmetry / audit logging.",
37	)
38	def logout():
39	    return {"message": "Logged out. Discard your access token."}
40	
41	
42	@auth_bp.get("/me")
43	@auth_bp.output(UserOut)
44	@auth_bp.auth_required(auth)
45	@auth_bp.doc(summary="Current user", description="Profile of the authenticated user.")
46	def me():
47	    return current_user()
48	
49	
50	@auth_bp.post("/change-password")
51	@auth_bp.input(ChangePasswordIn)
52	@auth_bp.output(MessageOut)
53	@auth_bp.auth_required(auth)
54	@auth_bp.doc(summary="Change own password")
55	def change_password(json_data: dict):
56	    user = current_user()
57	    auth_service().change_own_password(
58	        user.id, json_data["current_password"], json_data["new_password"]
59	    )
60	    get_db().commit()
61	    return {"message": "Password changed successfully."}
62

1	"""Chat endpoints: session CRUD, history load, and sending a message.
2	
3	Every route is gated by a coarse RBAC permission (all roles may chat); the
4	service enforces per-user ownership and returns 404 for foreign sessions.
5	
6	Two ways to send a message: a plain JSON request that returns both turns at once,
7	and an SSE endpoint that streams the assistant reply token-by-token (PLAN §3.5).
8	The streaming view validates and persists the user turn *before* opening the
9	response, so 404/422 still arrive as real HTTP status codes; only the assistant
10	tokens flow inside the event stream.
11	"""
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import json
15	import uuid
16	
17	from apiflask import APIBlueprint
18	from flask import Response, stream_with_context
19	
20	from app.domain.user import Permission
21	from app.interface.deps import auth, chat_service, current_user, get_db
22	from app.interface.permissions import require_permission
23	from app.interface.schemas import (
24	    ChatMessageOut,
25	    ChatSessionDetailOut,
26	    ChatSessionOut,
27	    CreateSessionIn,
28	    MessageOut,
29	    SendMessageIn,
30	    SendMessageOut,
31	)
32	
33	chat_bp = APIBlueprint("chat", __name__, url_prefix="/chat", tag="Chat")
34	
35	_message_schema = ChatMessageOut()
36	
37	
38	def _sse(event: str, data: dict) -> str:
39	    """One SSE frame: a named event plus a JSON payload (PLAN §3.5)."""
40	    return f"event: {event}\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\n\n"
41	
42	
43	@chat_bp.post("/sessions")
44	@chat_bp.auth_required(auth)
45	@chat_bp.input(CreateSessionIn)
46	@chat_bp.output(ChatSessionOut, status_code=201)
47	@chat_bp.doc(summary="Create a chat session")
48	@require_permission(Permission.USE_CHAT)
49	def create_session(json_data: dict):
50	    session = chat_service().create_session(current_user(), json_data.get("title"))
51	    get_db().commit()
52	    return session
53	
54	
55	@chat_bp.get("/sessions")
56	@chat_bp.auth_required(auth)
57	@chat_bp.output(ChatSessionOut(many=True))
58	@chat_bp.doc(summary="List own sessions", description="Most-recently-active first.")
59	@require_permission(Permission.MANAGE_OWN_CHATS)
60	def list_sessions():
61	    return chat_service().list_sessions(current_user())
62	
63	
64	@chat_bp.get("/sessions/<uuid:session_id>")
65	@chat_bp.auth_required(auth)
66	@chat_bp.output(ChatSessionDetailOut)
67	@chat_bp.doc(
68	    summary="Load a session with history",
69	    description="Returns the session and its messages in chronological order. "
70	    "404 if the session does not exist or belongs to another user.",
71	)
72	@require_permission(Permission.MANAGE_OWN_CHATS)
73	def get_session(session_id: uuid.UUID):
74	    return chat_service().get_session(current_user(), session_id)
75	
76	
77	@chat_bp.delete("/sessions/<uuid:session_id>")
78	@chat_bp.auth_required(auth)
79	@chat_bp.output(MessageOut)
80	@chat_bp.doc(summary="Delete own session", description="Cascades to its messages.")
81	@require_permission(Permission.MANAGE_OWN_CHATS)
82	def delete_session(session_id: uuid.UUID):
83	    chat_service().delete_session(current_user(), session_id)
84	    get_db().commit()
85	    return {"message": "Session deleted."}
86	
87	
88	@chat_bp.post("/sessions/<uuid:session_id>/messages")
89	@chat_bp.auth_required(auth)
90	@chat_bp.input(SendMessageIn)
91	@chat_bp.output(SendMessageOut, status_code=201)
92	@chat_bp.doc(
93	    summary="Send a message",
94	    description="Persists the user message, gets the assistant reply (mock LLM in "
95	    "this phase), persists it, and returns both. Streaming arrives in Day 5.",
96	)
97	@require_permission(Permission.USE_CHAT)
98	def send_message(session_id: uuid.UUID, json_data: dict):
99	    result = chat_service().send_message(current_user(), session_id, json_data["content"])
100	    get_db().commit()
101	    return result
102	
103	
104	@chat_bp.post("/sessions/<uuid:session_id>/messages/stream")
105	@chat_bp.auth_required(auth)
106	@chat_bp.input(SendMessageIn)
107	@chat_bp.doc(
108	    summary="Send a message (SSE streaming)",
109	    description=(
110	        "Persists the user turn, then streams the assistant reply as "
111	        "`text/event-stream`. Events: `meta` (the persisted user message + "
112	        "session title), repeated `token` (content deltas), and a terminal "
113	        "`done` (the persisted assistant message) or `error` (the partial reply "
114	        "that was still saved). Ownership/validation failures return 404/422 "
115	        "before the stream opens. Send the JWT as a Bearer header."
116	    ),
117	    responses={200: "Server-sent event stream of the assistant reply."},
118	)
119	@require_permission(Permission.USE_CHAT)
120	def stream_message(session_id: uuid.UUID, json_data: dict):
121	    svc = chat_service()
122	    db = get_db()
123	    # Validate + persist the user turn up front so 404/422 are real HTTP codes
124	    # and the user message is durable before the stream begins.
125	    handle = svc.open_stream(current_user(), session_id, json_data["content"])
126	    db.commit()
127	
128	    def generate():
129	        yield _sse(
130	            "meta",
131	            {
132	                "session_id": str(handle.session.id),
133	                "title": handle.session.title,
134	                "user_message": _message_schema.dump(handle.user_message),
135	            },
136	        )
137	        for event in svc.stream_reply(handle, db.commit):
138	            if event["type"] == "token":
139	                yield _sse("token", {"value": event["value"]})
140	            elif event["type"] == "done":
141	                yield _sse(
142	                    "done",
143	                    {"assistant_message": _message_schema.dump(event["assistant_message"])},
144	                )
145	            elif event["type"] == "error":
146	                yield _sse(
147	                    "error",
148	                    {
149	                        "message": event["message"],
150	                        "assistant_message": _message_schema.dump(
151	                            event["assistant_message"]
152	                        ),
153	                    },
154	                )
155	
156	    return Response(
157	        stream_with_context(generate()),
158	        mimetype="text/event-stream",
159	        headers={
160	            "Cache-Control": "no-cache",
161	            # Defeat proxy/worker response buffering so tokens flush immediately.
162	            "X-Accel-Buffering": "no",
163	        },
164	    )
165

1	"""Admin / Super Admin endpoints (PLAN §1.2 matrix).
2	
3	Each route carries a coarse `@require_permission` gate; the service performs the
4	fine-grained, target-role-dependent checks (e.g. only super_admin may toggle an
5	admin) and cross-role constraints.
6	"""
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import uuid
10	
11	from apiflask import APIBlueprint
12	
13	from app.domain.user import Permission
14	from app.interface.deps import auth, current_user, get_db, user_admin_service
15	from app.interface.permissions import require_permission
16	from app.interface.schemas import CreateUserIn, SetActiveIn, UserOut
17	
18	admin_bp = APIBlueprint("admin", __name__, url_prefix="/admin", tag="Admin")
19	
20	
21	@admin_bp.get("/users")
22	@admin_bp.auth_required(auth)
23	@admin_bp.output(UserOut(many=True))
24	@admin_bp.doc(summary="List all users")
25	@require_permission(Permission.LIST_USERS)
26	def list_users():
27	    return user_admin_service().list_users(current_user())

29	
30	@admin_bp.post("/users")
31	@admin_bp.auth_required(auth)
32	@admin_bp.input(CreateUserIn)
33	@admin_bp.output(UserOut, status_code=201)
34	@admin_bp.doc(
35	    summary="Create a user",
36	    description="admin may create role=user; super_admin may also create role=admin. "
37	    "super_admin cannot be created via the API.",
38	)
39	@require_permission(Permission.CREATE_USER)
40	def create_user(json_data: dict):
41	    user = user_admin_service().create_user(
42	        current_user(),
43	        username=json_data["username"],
44	        password=json_data["password"],
45	        role=json_data["role"],
46	    )

51	@admin_bp.patch("/users/<uuid:user_id>/active")

53	@admin_bp.input(SetActiveIn)
54	@admin_bp.output(UserOut)
55	@admin_bp.doc(
56	    summary="Activate / deactivate a user",

58	    "super_admin accounts cannot be modified.",
59	)
60	@require_permission(Permission.TOGGLE_USER_ACTIVE)
61	def set_active(user_id: uuid.UUID, json_data: dict):
62	    user = user_admin_service().set_active(current_user(), user_id, json_data["is_active"])
63	    get_db().commit()
64	    return user
65	
66	
67	@admin_bp.post("/users/<uuid:user_id>/promote")
68	@admin_bp.auth_required(auth)
69	@admin_bp.output(UserOut)
70	@admin_bp.doc(summary="Promote a user to admin", description="super_admin only.")
71	@require_permission(Permission.PROMOTE_USER_TO_ADMIN)
72	def promote(user_id: uuid.UUID):
73	    user = user_admin_service().promote_to_admin(current_user(), user_id)
74	    get_db().commit()
75	    return user

"""Marshmallow schemas for request/response validation and OpenAPI docs."""
from __future__ import annotations

from apiflask import Schema
from apiflask.fields import Boolean, DateTime, Enum, Nested, String
from apiflask.validators import Length

from app.domain.chat import MessageRole
from app.domain.user import Role


# ---- Auth ----
class LoginIn(Schema):
    username = String(required=True, validate=Length(min=1, max=64))
    password = String(required=True, validate=Length(min=1))


class TokenOut(Schema):
    access_token = String()
    token_type = String()


class ChangePasswordIn(Schema):
    current_password = String(required=True, validate=Length(min=1))
    new_password = String(required=True, validate=Length(min=8, max=256))


class MessageOut(Schema):
    message = String()


# ---- Admin: user management ----
class CreateUserIn(Schema):
    username = String(required=True, validate=Length(min=1, max=64))
    password = String(required=True, validate=Length(min=8, max=256))
    # super_admin is intentionally not creatable via the API (seed only).
    role = Enum(Role, by_value=True, load_default=Role.USER)


class SetActiveIn(Schema):
    is_active = Boolean(required=True)


# ---- User ----
class UserOut(Schema):
    id = String()
    username = String()
    role = Enum(Role, by_value=True)  # serialize to the enum value, e.g. "super_admin"
    is_active = Boolean()
    created_at = DateTime()
    updated_at = DateTime()


# ---- Chat ----
class CreateSessionIn(Schema):
    # Optional: a session with no title is auto-named from its first message.
    title = String(required=False, validate=Length(max=200))


class ChatSessionOut(Schema):
    """List item — no messages, keeps the left-side list query off the N+1 path."""

    id = String()
    title = String(allow_none=True)
    created_at = DateTime()
    updated_at = DateTime()


class ChatMessageOut(Schema):
    id = String()
    role = Enum(MessageRole, by_value=True)
    content = String()
    created_at = DateTime()


class ChatSessionDetailOut(ChatSessionOut):
    """A session with its full message history (chronological)."""

    messages = Nested(ChatMessageOut, many=True)


class SendMessageIn(Schema):
    content = String(required=True, validate=Length(min=1, max=8000))


class SendMessageOut(Schema):
    user_message = Nested(ChatMessageOut)
    assistant_message = Nested(ChatMessageOut)

1	"""Application factory (apiflask).
2	
3	Wires config, OpenAPI `/docs`, blueprints, request-scoped DB, and a uniform
4	mapping from application errors to HTTP responses (business rules return 4xx,
5	not 500). Chat/admin blueprints are registered here as they land in later phases.
6	"""
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	from apiflask import APIFlask
10	from flask import g
11	
12	from app.application.errors import AppError
13	from app.infrastructure.config import Settings, get_settings
14	
15	
16	def create_app(settings: Settings | None = None) -> APIFlask:
17	    settings = settings or get_settings()
18	
19	    app = APIFlask(
20	        __name__,
21	        title="LLM Chat API",
22	        version="0.1.0",
23	        docs_path="/docs",
24	    )
25	    app.config["DESCRIPTION"] = "Multi-user LLM chat web application API."
26	    app.config["AUTO_404_RESPONSE"] = True
27	
28	    from app.interface import deps
29	
30	    deps.init_app(app)
31	    _register_blueprints(app)
32	    _register_error_handlers(app)
33	
34	    return app
35	
36	
37	def _register_blueprints(app: APIFlask) -> None:
38	    from app.interface.api.admin import admin_bp
39	    from app.interface.api.auth import auth_bp
40	    from app.interface.api.chat import chat_bp
41	    from app.interface.api.health import health_bp
42

47	
48

50	    @app.errorhandler(AppError)
51	    def _handle_app_error(exc: AppError):
52	        # A failed business rule must not leave a half-applied transaction.
53	        db = g.pop("db", None)
54	        if db is not None:
55	            db.rollback()
56	            db.close()
57	        return {"message": exc.message}, exc.status_code

file.content:
"""Application factory (apiflask).

Wires config, OpenAPI `/docs`, blueprints, request-scoped DB, and a uniform
mapping from application errors to HTTP responses (business rules return 4xx,
not 500). Chat/admin blueprints are registered here as they land in later phases.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from apiflask import APIFlask
from flask import g

from app.application.errors import AppError
from app.infrastructure.config import Settings, get_settings


def create_app(settings: Settings | None = None) -> APIFlask:
    settings = settings or get_settings()

    app = APIFlask(
        __name__,
        title="LLM Chat API",
        version="0.1.0",
        docs_path="/docs",
    )
    app.config["DESCRIPTION"] = "Multi-user LLM chat web application API."
    app.config["AUTO_404_RESPONSE"] = True

    from app.interface import deps

    deps.init_app(app)
    _register_blueprints(app)
    _register_error_handlers(app)

    return app


def _register_blueprints(app: APIFlask) -> None:
    from app.interface.api.admin import admin_bp
    from app.interface.api.auth import auth_bp
    from app.interface.api.chat import chat_bp
    from app.interface.api.health import health_bp

    app.register_blueprint(health_bp)
    app.register_blueprint(auth_bp)
    app.register_blueprint(admin_bp)
    app.register_blueprint(chat_bp)


def _register_error_handlers(app: APIFlask) -> None:
    @app.errorhandler(AppError)
    def _handle_app_error(exc: AppError):
        # A failed business rule must not leave a half-applied transaction.
        db = g.pop("db", None)
        if db is not None:
            db.rollback()
            db.close()
        return {"message": exc.message}, exc.status_code

1	"""Centralised application configuration.
2	
3	All secrets and tunables are read from environment variables via
4	pydantic-settings — there is zero hardcoding of credentials. This is the single
5	source of truth imported across the app (DB URL, JWT secret, LLM keys, seed).
6	"""
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	from functools import lru_cache
10	
11	from pydantic import Field, computed_field
12	from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
13	
14	
15	class Settings(BaseSettings):
16	    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
17	        env_file=".env",
18	        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
19	        extra="ignore",
20	        case_sensitive=False,
21	    )
22	
23	    # ---- App ----
24	    app_env: str = "development"
25	    log_level: str = "INFO"
26	
27	    # ---- Database ----
28	    postgres_user: str = "chat"
29	    postgres_password: str = "chat"
30	    postgres_db: str = "chatdb"
31	    postgres_host: str = "db"
32	    postgres_port: int = 5432
33	    # Optional explicit override; if set, takes precedence over the parts above.
34	    database_url: str | None = None
35	
36	    # ---- JWT / security ----
37	    jwt_secret: str = Field(default="dev-insecure-secret-change-me")
38	    jwt_algorithm: str = "HS256"
39	    jwt_access_token_expires_minutes: int = 120
40	
41	    # ---- Super admin seed (invariant I-1) ----
42	    super_admin_username: str | None = None
43	    super_admin_password: str | None = None
44	
45	    # ---- LLM ----
46	    llm_provider: str = "mock"  # groq | mock
47	    groq_api_key: str | None = None
48	    groq_base_url: str = "https://api.groq.com/openai/v1"
49	    llm_model: str = "llama-3.3-70b-versatile"
50	    llm_title_model: str = "llama-3.1-8b-instant"
51	
52	    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
53	    @property
54	    def sqlalchemy_database_uri(self) -> str:
55	        if self.database_url:
56	            return self.database_url
57	        return (
58	            f"postgresql+psycopg://{self.postgres_user}:{self.postgres_password}"
59	            f"@{self.postgres_host}:{self.postgres_port}/{self.postgres_db}"
60	        )
61	
62	    @property
63	    def is_production(self) -> bool:
64	        return self.app_env.lower() == "production"
65	
66	
67	@lru_cache
68	def get_settings() -> Settings:
69	    """Cached accessor so the env is parsed once per process."""
70	    return Settings()
71

file.content:
"""Centralised application configuration.

All secrets and tunables are read from environment variables via
pydantic-settings — there is zero hardcoding of credentials. This is the single
source of truth imported across the app (DB URL, JWT secret, LLM keys, seed).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from functools import lru_cache

from pydantic import Field, computed_field
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
        env_file=".env",
        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
        extra="ignore",
        case_sensitive=False,
    )

    # ---- App ----
    app_env: str = "development"
    log_level: str = "INFO"

    # ---- Database ----
    postgres_user: str = "chat"
    postgres_password: str = "chat"
    postgres_db: str = "chatdb"
    postgres_host: str = "db"
    postgres_port: int = 5432
    # Optional explicit override; if set, takes precedence over the parts above.
    database_url: str | None = None

    # ---- JWT / security ----
    jwt_secret: str = Field(default="dev-insecure-secret-change-me")
    jwt_algorithm: str = "HS256"
    jwt_access_token_expires_minutes: int = 120

    # ---- Super admin seed (invariant I-1) ----
    super_admin_username: str | None = None
    super_admin_password: str | None = None

    # ---- LLM ----
    llm_provider: str = "mock"  # groq | mock
    groq_api_key: str | None = None
    groq_base_url: str = "https://api.groq.com/openai/v1"
    llm_model: str = "llama-3.3-70b-versatile"
    llm_title_model: str = "llama-3.1-8b-instant"

    @computed_field  # type: ignore[prop-decorator]
    @property
    def sqlalchemy_database_uri(self) -> str:
        if self.database_url:
            return self.database_url
        return (
            f"postgresql+psycopg://{self.postgres_user}:{self.postgres_password}"
            f"@{self.postgres_host}:{self.postgres_port}/{self.postgres_db}"
        )

    @property
    def is_production(self) -> bool:
        return self.app_env.lower() == "production"


@lru_cache
def get_settings() -> Settings:
    """Cached accessor so the env is parsed once per process."""
    return Settings()

# One-command-up stack: PostgreSQL + API.
# Frontend service is added in a later phase (PLAN Day 5/6).
#   docker compose up --build
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB}
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER} -d ${POSTGRES_DB}"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10
    # Exposed for local debugging; remove in production.
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"

  api:
    build:
      context: ./backend
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    restart: unless-stopped
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      # Inside the compose network the DB is reachable at service name "db".
      POSTGRES_HOST: db
      POSTGRES_PORT: "5432"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "python -c \"import urllib.request,sys; sys.exit(0 if urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:8000/health').status==200 else 1)\""]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 20s

volumes:
  pgdata:

content.content:
1	"""Dependency wiring for the interface layer.
2	
3	Provides a request-scoped DB session, the Bearer-token authenticator (decodes
4	JWT -> loads the active user), and small factories that assemble services with
5	the request's repositories. Views stay thin; swapping a fake repo/LLM in tests
6	is just a different factory.
7	"""
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from apiflask import HTTPTokenAuth
11	from flask import Flask, g
12	from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
13	
14	from app.application.auth_service import AuthService
15	from app.application.chat_service import ChatService
16	from app.application.user_admin_service import UserAdminService
17	from app.infrastructure.config import get_settings
18	from app.infrastructure.db.models import User
19	from app.infrastructure.db.repositories import SqlChatRepository, SqlUserRepository
20	from app.infrastructure.db.session import SessionLocal
21	from app.infrastructure.llm import get_llm_provider
22	from app.infrastructure.security.jwt import TokenError, decode_access_token
23	
24	auth = HTTPTokenAuth(scheme="Bearer", description="JWT access token from /auth/login")
25	
26	
27	def get_db() -> Session:
28	    if "db" not in g:
29	        g.db = SessionLocal()
30	    return g.db
31	
32	
33	def user_repository() -> SqlUserRepository:
34	    return SqlUserRepository(get_db())
35	
36	
37	def auth_service() -> AuthService:
38	    return AuthService(user_repository())
39	
40	
41	def user_admin_service() -> UserAdminService:
42	    return UserAdminService(user_repository())
43	
44	
45	def chat_repository() -> SqlChatRepository:
46	    return SqlChatRepository(get_db())
47	
48	
49	def chat_service() -> ChatService:
50	    return ChatService(chat_repository(), get_llm_provider(get_settings()))
51	
52	
53	@auth.verify_token
54	def verify_token(token: str) -> User | None:
55	    """Decode the JWT and load the active user, or None to reject (401)."""
56	    try:
57	        claims = decode_access_token(token)
58	    except TokenError:
59	        return None
60	    user = user_repository().get_by_id(claims.user_id)
61	    if user is None or not user.is_active:
62	        return None
63	    return user
64	
65	
66	def current_user() -> User:
67	    """The authenticated user for the current request."""
68	    return auth.current_user
69	
70	
71	def init_app(app: Flask) -> None:
72	    @app.teardown_appcontext
73	    def _close_db(_exc: BaseException | None) -> None:
74	        db = g.pop("db", None)
75	        if db is not None:
76	            db.close()
77

file.content:
"""User domain: roles and the RBAC permission matrix.

This module is pure business logic — it imports no framework. The permission
matrix is the single source of truth driving authorisation (PLAN §3.3); views
check against it via a decorator rather than scattering role checks.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import enum


class Role(str, enum.Enum):
    USER = "user"
    ADMIN = "admin"
    SUPER_ADMIN = "super_admin"


class Permission(str, enum.Enum):
    # Self-service (all roles)
    CHANGE_OWN_PASSWORD = "change_own_password"
    USE_CHAT = "use_chat"
    MANAGE_OWN_CHATS = "manage_own_chats"

    # Admin tier
    CREATE_USER = "create_user"            # create role=user
    LIST_USERS = "list_users"
    TOGGLE_USER_ACTIVE = "toggle_user_active"        # activate/deactivate role=user

    # Super-admin tier
    CREATE_ADMIN = "create_admin"          # create role=admin
    TOGGLE_ADMIN_ACTIVE = "toggle_admin_active"      # activate/deactivate role=admin
    PROMOTE_USER_TO_ADMIN = "promote_user_to_admin"
    EXPORT_ALL_CHATS = "export_all_chats"


# Single source of truth: Permission -> roles that hold it (PLAN §1.2 matrix).
PERMISSION_MATRIX: dict[Permission, frozenset[Role]] = {
    Permission.CHANGE_OWN_PASSWORD: frozenset({Role.USER, Role.ADMIN, Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
    Permission.USE_CHAT: frozenset({Role.USER, Role.ADMIN, Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
    Permission.MANAGE_OWN_CHATS: frozenset({Role.USER, Role.ADMIN, Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
    Permission.CREATE_USER: frozenset({Role.ADMIN, Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
    Permission.LIST_USERS: frozenset({Role.ADMIN, Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
    Permission.TOGGLE_USER_ACTIVE: frozenset({Role.ADMIN, Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
    Permission.CREATE_ADMIN: frozenset({Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
    Permission.TOGGLE_ADMIN_ACTIVE: frozenset({Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
    Permission.PROMOTE_USER_TO_ADMIN: frozenset({Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
    Permission.EXPORT_ALL_CHATS: frozenset({Role.SUPER_ADMIN}),
}


def role_has_permission(role: Role, permission: Permission) -> bool:
    return role in PERMISSION_MATRIX.get(permission, frozenset())

content.content:
1	# LLM Chat Web App
2	
3	Multi-user LLM chat web application — apiflask (backend) + Vue 3 (frontend) +
4	PostgreSQL + Groq, one-command up with Docker Compose.
5	
6	> Build follows [PLAN.md](PLAN.md). This README grows each phase; current state:
7	> **Day 5 — real Groq SSE streaming** (token-by-token assistant replies, mock
8	> still available offline).
9	
10	## Quick start
11	
12	```bash
13	# 1. Create your env file from the template and fill in secrets.
14	cp .env.example .env
15	# Generate a strong JWT secret:
16	python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(48))"
17	# Set JWT_SECRET, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME/PASSWORD in .env.
18	
19	# 2. Bring up the stack (Postgres + API). Migrations run automatically.
20	docker compose up --build
21	```
22	
23	Then open:
24	
25	- API docs (OpenAPI / Swagger UI): http://localhost:8000/docs
26	- Liveness:  http://localhost:8000/health
27	- Readiness (checks DB): http://localhost:8000/health/ready
28	
29	## Project layout
30	
31	```
32	backend/                 apiflask service (Clean Architecture, PLAN §3.1)
33	  app/
34	    domain/              pure business: Role, RBAC matrix, message roles
35	    application/         use-case / service layer (added Day 2+)
36	    infrastructure/      config, db (SQLAlchemy + Alembic), security, llm
37	    interface/           apiflask blueprints + schemas
38	  alembic/               migrations
39	  Dockerfile            multi-stage, non-root runtime
40	docker-compose.yml       db + api (frontend added later)
41	.env.example             complete environment template (no secrets committed)
42	```
43	
44	## Tech choices & rationale
45	
46	See [PLAN.md §2](PLAN.md). Summary:
47	
48	- **PostgreSQL** — production-grade, mature SQLAlchemy + Alembic ecosystem,
49	  native JSONB; the extra service is covered by compose so one-command-up holds.
50	- **Groq** — free tier, OpenAI-compatible API, native SSE streaming. Wrapped
51	  behind an `LLMProvider` port so the provider can be swapped via config.
52	- **JWT HS256** — single issuer / single service; HS256 + env secret is the
53	  simplest sufficient choice (RS256 would be over-engineering here).
54	- **Argon2id** — modern, memory-hard password hashing.
55	- **Gunicorn + gevent worker** — SSE streams are long-lived I/O waits;
56	  gevent keeps workers from being tied up per stream.
57	
58	## Authentication
59	
60	- **Default admin (seed):** on first startup an idempotent seed creates the
61	  `super_admin` from `SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME` / `SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD`. If either
62	  is missing the app **fails fast** rather than booting with no administrator
63	  (invariant I-1). Re-running is a no-op (never rebuilds/overwrites).
64	- **Login:** `POST /auth/login` with `{username, password}` returns a JWT
65	  access token. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on protected routes.
66	- **Logout:** `POST /auth/logout` — JWT is stateless, so logout is client-side
67	  token disposal; the endpoint exists for symmetry and audit logging.
68	- **Change own password:** `POST /auth/change-password`.
69	- **Passwords** are hashed with Argon2id, never stored in plaintext.
70	
71	Quick demo (after `docker compose up`):
72	
73	```bash
74	TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/auth/login \
75	  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
76	  -d '{"username":"superadmin","password":"<your SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD>"}' \
77	  | python -c "import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)['access_token'])")
78	curl -s localhost:8000/auth/me -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
79	```
80	
81	## Authorization (RBAC)
82	
83	Three roles — `user` / `admin` / `super_admin` — driven by a single permission
84	matrix in [domain/user.py](backend/app/domain/user.py). Views carry a
85	`@require_permission(...)` gate; fine-grained, target-dependent rules live in the
86	service layer (so business rules return 4xx, not 500).
87	
88	Admin / Super Admin API:
89	
90	| Method & path | Permission | admin | super_admin |
91	|---|---|:---:|:---:|
92	| `GET  /admin/users` | list users | ✓ | ✓ |
93	| `POST /admin/users` (role=user) | create user | ✓ | ✓ |
94	| `POST /admin/users` (role=admin) | create admin | ✗ | ✓ |
95	| `PATCH /admin/users/{id}/active` (user) | toggle user | ✓ | ✓ |
96	| `PATCH /admin/users/{id}/active` (admin) | toggle admin | ✗ | ✓ |
97	| `POST /admin/users/{id}/promote` | promote user→admin | ✗ | ✓ |
98	
99	**Invariant I-2 — always ≥ 1 active super_admin:** guaranteed structurally.
100	No API path creates, deactivates, or demotes a super_admin (they exist only via
101	the seed), so the system can never reach a "no super admin" state. Any operation
102	targeting a super_admin is rejected (403).
103	
104	## Chat
105	
106	Each user has multiple chat sessions (ChatGPT-style). Sessions and messages are
107	persisted; history loads in chronological order. Authorization here is **per-user
108	ownership** — a session belonging to another user returns `404` (not `403`) so
109	the API never reveals which session ids exist.
110	
111	| Method & path | Description |
112	|---|---|
113	| `POST   /chat/sessions` | create a session (optional `title`) |
114	| `GET    /chat/sessions` | list own sessions, most-recently-active first |
115	| `GET    /chat/sessions/{id}` | load a session with its full message history |
116	| `DELETE /chat/sessions/{id}` | delete own session (cascades to messages) |
117	| `POST   /chat/sessions/{id}/messages` | send a message; persists the user turn, gets the assistant reply, persists it, returns both (non-streaming) |
118	| `POST   /chat/sessions/{id}/messages/stream` | same, but streams the assistant reply token-by-token over SSE |
119	
120	- **LLM provider** is abstracted behind an `LLMProvider` port with two
121	  implementations: a deterministic offline **mock** (`LLM_PROVIDER=mock`, works
122	  with no API key) and the real **Groq** adapter (`LLM_PROVIDER=groq` +
123	  `GROQ_API_KEY`), an OpenAI-compatible client so swapping providers is config,
124	  not code. `LLM_PROVIDER=groq` with no key **fails fast**.
125	- **Streaming (SSE).** The stream endpoint persists the user turn and commits it
126	  *before* opening the response (so ownership/validation errors are real
127	  `404`/`422`, and the user turn survives a dropped client), then emits
128	  `text/event-stream` events: a `meta` frame (persisted user message + session
129	  title), repeated `token` frames (content deltas), and a terminal `done`
130	  (persisted assistant message) or `error`. On an upstream failure mid-stream the
131	  partial reply received so far is still persisted, so a user turn never dangles
132	  without an answer (PLAN §3.5). Pass the JWT as a Bearer header. The served
133	  reply concatenates the deltas to exactly what the non-streaming path returns.
134	- **First message** auto-names an untitled session (truncated); the title is not
135	  overwritten afterwards.
136	- **Ordering** does not trust the wall clock (Postgres `now()` is
137	  transaction-time, and OS clocks can be coarse): each message is stamped
138	  strictly after the last in its session, so history is deterministic without an
139	  extra sequence column.
140	
141	```bash
142	SID=$(curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/chat/sessions \
143	  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
144	  -d '{}' | python -c "import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])")
145	curl -s -X POST localhost:8000/chat/sessions/$SID/messages \
146	  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
147	  -d '{"content":"Hello!"}'
148	
149	# Streaming variant — -N disables curl's buffering so tokens print as they arrive:
150	curl -N -X POST localhost:8000/chat/sessions/$SID/messages/stream \
151	  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
152	  -d '{"content":"Tell me a short joke."}'
153	```
154	
155	## Configuration
156	
157	All secrets and tunables come from environment variables (`.env`); nothing is
158	hardcoded. See [.env.example](.env.example) for the full list. `.env` is
159	git-ignored and docker-ignored.
160	
161	## Testing
162	
163	Unit tests use a fake repository (no DB) for services and in-memory SQLite for
164	seed/repository tests — fast and deterministic.
165	
166	```bash
167	cd backend
168	pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
169	pytest
170	```
171	
172	## Status / roadmap
173	
174	- [x] **Day 1** — foundation: skeleton, config, DB + Alembic migration, `/docs`,
175	  health checks, Docker Compose one-command-up.
176	- [x] **Day 2** — auth: Argon2 hashing, JWT (HS256), login/logout/me/change
177	  password, super_admin idempotent seed with fail-fast, unit tests.
178	- [x] **Day 3** — RBAC: central permission matrix + `@require_permission`,
179	  admin/super-admin API (create/list/activate/promote), cross-role guards,
180	  invariant I-2 (always >=1 active super_admin), full matrix tests.
181	- [x] **Day 4** — chat persistence: session CRUD, chronological message history,
182	  per-user ownership (404 on foreign sessions), `LLMProvider` port + mock,
183	  auto-title on first message; service + repository tests.
184	- [x] **Day 5** — real Groq SSE streaming: `LLMProvider.stream`, Groq adapter
185	  (OpenAI-compatible, fail-fast on missing key), SSE endpoint with token frames,
186	  partial-persist on dropped upstream; gevent worker already configured for the
187	  long-lived connections. Service, factory, and end-to-end stream tests.
188	- [ ] Day 6 — export, frontend admin pages, observability bonuses.
189	- [ ] Day 7 — docs, transcript redaction, demo.
190
